patient_history.chief_complaint: acute complete vision loss in the right eye after endoscopic sinus surgery
known_conditions: prior endoscopic sinus surgery about 15 years earlier

patient_transcript:
Doctor: Hello, what brings you in today?
Patient: I lost vision in my right eye after sinus surgery yesterday.
Doctor: Did the vision loss happen immediately after the surgery?
Patient: Yes. Right after the operation I had no light perception in that eye.
Doctor: Have you had sinus surgery before?
Patient: Yes, I had endoscopic sinus surgery about 15 years ago and did not have any vision problems then.
Doctor: Were any scans done at the outside clinic?
Patient: Yes, an orbital CT showed a small fracture in the medial wall on the right and a thick bony septum in the ethmoid area.
Doctor: Since admission we started steroid pulse therapy because we are worried about a severe postoperative orbital or optic nerve injury.
Patient: Yes.
Doctor: What did the other tests show?
Patient: The visual evoked potential test showed no right optic nerve activity, and the MRI showed enhancement around the posterior optic nerve sheath with an abnormal focus near the medial rectus close to the orbital apex.
Doctor: Has your vision improved at all after treatment?
Patient: No, I still cannot perceive light in the right eye.
chart_notes: 53-year-old woman admitted 1 day after revision right endoscopic sinus surgery performed under local anesthesia for acute postoperative right monocular blindness. Immediately after surgery the right eye had no light perception. She previously underwent endoscopic sinus surgery about 15 years earlier without visual complications. Outside orbital CT showed a small right medial orbital wall fracture and a thick bony septum in the right ethmoid region. Empiric IV methylprednisolone pulse therapy was started for suspected postoperative optic nerve injury and was followed by oral prednisolone. Visual evoked potential testing showed absent right optic nerve activity; orbital MRI demonstrated mild enhancement along the posterior right optic nerve sheath and an abnormal dark signal near the medial rectus at the orbital apex. Vision did not recover after 2 weeks of steroids and remained absent at 2-month follow-up.
